examples: WIND BURN TR. BET. WELLS FARGO & TRAPPERS RDG -> Wind Burn Tr. Bet. Wells Fargo & Trappers Rdg
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 6269 of 6269 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 24488 of 24488 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 5894 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.what: 359 of 24488 trips have no headsign.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.effort: One field.
findings.fix: Add feed_contact_email to feed_info.txt.
findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_diff_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance diff coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_timepoint_value' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing timepoint value (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_diff_coordinates_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance diff coordinates distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
findings.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
findings.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged trip.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
findings.what: Some scheduled trips travel implausibly fast between stops.
findings.why: Usually a typo'd stop time; riders get impossible arrival estimates.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already expired.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_stop' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Same name and description for stop (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One field per route.
findings.fix: Pick a darker/lighter route_text_color for the flagged routes.
findings.what: Some route colors don't contrast with their text color.
findings.why: Route badges become unreadable, especially for riders with low vision.

findings.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
findings.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
findings.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_headsign_matches_intermediate_stop' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip headsign matches intermediate stop (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 16 kinds of issue across 1165782 instances (27 error, 1165533 warning, 222 informational).

findings.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
findings.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
findings.what: Service data ended 789 day(s) ago.
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data ended 789 day(s) ago. Trip planners have likely already dropped this feed.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 1 validator error to resolve. Service data expired 788 days ago. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Your feed's agency_id is 1; your National Transit Database ID is 60011. A feed that serves several agencies (a shared regional feed) can legitimately carry more than one agency_id, so a difference here is a heads-up, not an error.
ntd_id_alignment.fix: Optionally set the agency_id for your service to 60011 in agency.txt (and the matching agency_id in routes.txt) so the feed lines up with your NTD record. It is a convenience, not a required feed change: FTA also links agency_id to your NTD ID on your P-50 form.

pillars.detail: Service data expired 788 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

ntd_readiness.summary: Resolve this before you certify on the D-10. 1 validator error to resolve. Service data expired 788 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 54 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (24, 26, 21, 22, 8, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 5011 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("S.W. 35TH & OLGA", "Blanco & Mt Capote", "WIND BURN TR. BET. WELLS FARGO & TRAPPERS RDG", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

findings.effort: A cleanup pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove stops no route serves, or add the trips that should call at them.
findings.what: 43 of 6269 boardable stops are never served by any trip.
findings.why: Riders see these stops in apps and on the map but can never catch anything there, which erodes trust in the data.

top_fixes.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
top_fixes.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_diff_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Equal shape distance diff coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 6269 of 6269 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.
